Data

Data report · Florida DBPR public inspection records · June 2026

Loved but Failing

77 Central Florida restaurants keep a 4.5-star diner rating or better — and carry a D or F health grade anyway. 43 of them are failing outright. Each bar below is the distance between what the crowd says and what the inspector found. The widest gaps come first.

4.6★ 922 diner reviews
vs

A high rating is a verdict on the food. A health grade is a verdict on the kitchen. This list is every Central Florida restaurant we track where the two disagree most — 43 graded F, 34 graded D, all rated 4.5 stars or higher. Every grade is calculated from each restaurant’s most recent Florida inspection; tap any name for the full violation record.

More data reports

  • Reference The Safety Map Every county graded, cleanest to riskiest.
  • Counterintuitive The Disney Paradox The cleanest kitchens in Central Florida are inside Walt Disney World.
  • Counterintuitive Cuisines, Ranked Which cuisines grade cleanest — and which really don’t.
  • Trajectories Comebacks & Collapses The biggest health turnarounds and freefalls on record.
  • Enforcement Emergency Orders Where the state took its strongest action.

Source: Florida DBPR public inspection records, as of June 2026. Grades are calculated by us from each restaurant’s most recent inspection — Florida does not issue letter grades. Diner ratings and review counts are third-party. How we calculate grades →
